skill.name: Mermaid Expert
skill.description: Create Mermaid diagrams for flowcharts, sequences, ERDs, and architectures
skill.summary: Expert guidance for creating professional Mermaid.js visualizations and system diagrams
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 lines and detected 0 potential security issues. This is a prompt-only skill that provides guidance on creating Mermaid.js diagrams. No executable code, network access, filesystem operations, or external commands are present. The skill poses no security risks and is safe for publication.

content.value_statement: Transform complex processes and systems into clear visual diagrams without learning Mermaid syntax. Get professional, readable charts for documentation and presentations.
actual_capabilities: Generate flowcharts and decision trees with proper Mermaid syntax
Create sequence diagrams for API interactions and system workflows
Design entity relationship diagrams (ERD) for database schemas
Build state diagrams, user journeys, and Gantt charts
Apply consistent styling and accessibility best practices
Provide both basic and styled diagram versions with explanations
limitations: Cannot render diagrams directly - requires Mermaid-compatible viewer or editor
Complex diagrams with many nodes may become difficult to read
Diagram rendering depends on the Mermaid version used by the host platform
Cannot interact with external diagram tools or export files directly

use_cases.title: Technical Documentation
use_cases.description: Generate system architecture diagrams and process flowcharts for documentation

use_cases.title: API Design Reviews
use_cases.description: Create sequence diagrams to visualize API call flows and integration patterns

use_cases.title: Project Planning
use_cases.description: Build Gantt charts and timelines for project management and stakeholder updates

prompt_templates.title: Basic Flowchart
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a simple flowchart showing the steps of [describe your process]. Use basic shapes and clear labels.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - Quick diagram for basic process visualization

prompt_templates.title: Sequence Diagram
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a sequence diagram showing the interaction between [list components] when [describe the scenario]. Include request and response messages.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - API or system interaction documentation

prompt_templates.title: Entity Relationship Diagram
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an ERD for a [type of application] with entities for [list main entities]. Show relationships, cardinality, and key attributes.

prompt_templates.title: Styled Architecture Diagram
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a styled architecture diagram showing [describe system]. Use custom colors, icons, and subgraphs to organize components by layer or service.

output_examples.input: Create a flowchart for user login process
output_examples.output: A complete Mermaid flowchart with nodes for 'User enters credentials', 'System validates', 'Grant access' or 'Show error', connected with directional arrows and decision diamonds for validation outcomes.

output_examples.input: Show API sequence diagram for payment processing
output_examples.output: A sequence diagram with participants (Customer, Frontend, API, Payment Gateway, Database) showing message flow: initiate payment, validate request, process charge, confirm transaction, update order status.

best_practices: Keep diagrams focused - limit to 15-20 nodes for readability
Use consistent colors and shapes to represent similar element types
Add comments in Mermaid code to explain complex relationships or syntax
anti_patterns: Overcrowding diagrams with too many nodes or crossing connections
Using inconsistent styling that confuses diagram interpretation
Creating diagrams without clear purpose or audience consideration

faq.question: Where can I view Mermaid diagrams?
faq.answer: Mermaid diagrams render in GitHub, Notion, Obsidian, and many documentation tools. You can also use the Mermaid Live Editor at mermaid.live for testing and sharing.

faq.question: What diagram types does this skill support?
faq.answer: Flowcharts, sequence diagrams, class diagrams, state diagrams, ERDs, Gantt charts, pie charts, git graphs, user journeys, quadrant charts, and timelines.

faq.question: Can this skill export diagrams as images?
faq.answer: The skill provides Mermaid code that you can render. Export to PNG, SVG, or PDF requires using Mermaid-compatible tools or the Mermaid CLI.

faq.question: How do I include Mermaid diagrams in my README?
faq.answer: GitHub supports Mermaid natively. Wrap your Mermaid code in triple backticks with 'mermaid' as the language identifier, like a code block.

faq.question: What if my diagram is too complex?
faq.answer: Split large diagrams into multiple focused diagrams. Use subgraphs to organize related elements. Prioritize clarity over showing every detail.

faq.question: Does this skill work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is compatible with all three AI assistants. Simply invoke the skill when you need help creating or understanding Mermaid diagrams.
